message:
Extract connector implementations to franken_agent_detection crate

Major refactoring to move agent detection logic to a shared crate:

Connector extraction:
- Gut claude_code.rs, replacing 1460 lines with a 3-line compatibility
  shim pointing to franken_agent_detection as the canonical implementation
- Slim connectors/mod.rs by removing inline implementations and re-exporting
  from franken_agent_detection instead, including new token extraction APIs
  (ExtractedTokenUsage, ModelInfo, TokenDataSource, etc.)
- Update lib.rs re-exports to include new token extraction public API

Dependency updates:
- Add/update franken_agent_detection dependency in Cargo.toml
- Refresh Cargo.lock

Search and UI improvements:
- Refine search/query.rs query processing and ranking logic
- Update pages/preview.rs rendering with improved formatting
- Enhance ui/app.rs with refined navigation and display logic
- Update ui/analytics_charts.rs chart rendering
- Improve tui_asciicast.rs terminal recording display
- Refine update_check.rs version checking

Other updates:
- Update daemon/resource.rs resource management
- Adjust sources/sync.rs synchronization logic
- Update fastembed_embedder.rs embedding configuration
- Refresh theme components
- Update AGENTS.md, beads tracking
- Adjust tests for refactored connector API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.11","title":"FAD: Move cline.rs connector to franken_agent_detection","description":"WHAT: Move src/connectors/cline.rs (1108 lines) to FAD.

CLINE CONNECTOR: Parses Cline (VS Code extension) session files from VS Code globalStorage. Navigates the .cline directory structure, parsing task directories with conversation data.

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 1-4

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
- VS Code extension storage path detection (shares pattern with copilot, amp)
- May have complex workspace tracking logic
- Consider extracting shared VS Code path detection into a utility used by cline, copilot, and amp

Follow migration pattern from FAD task 5.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- ClineConnector compiles in FAD
- VS Code path detection works cross-platform

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.12","title":"FAD: Move amp.rs connector to franken_agent_detection","description":"WHAT: Move src/connectors/amp.rs (1291 lines) to FAD.

AMP CONNECTOR: Parses Amp (Sourcegraph) session data from ~/.local/share/amp and VS Code extension storage. Dual-source detection (standalone app + VS Code extension).

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 1-4

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
- Dual-source: standalone app directory AND VS Code extension storage
- VS Code path detection shared with copilot, cline
- Agent metadata extraction specific to Amp's format
- May have platform-specific paths for standalone app

Follow migration pattern from FAD task 5.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AmpConnector compiles in FAD
- Both standalone and VS Code sources detected

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.13","title":"FAD: Move pi_agent.rs connector to franken_agent_detection","description":"WHAT: Move src/connectors/pi_agent.rs (1395 lines) to FAD.

PI AGENT CONNECTOR: Parses Pi Agent session files from ~/.pi/agent/sessions/. Rich event structure with typed events: session_start, message, model_change, thinking_level_change. Extracts extended thinking content, flattens tool calls with arguments, tracks model changes.

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 1-4

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
- Uses PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR env var override (already handled by FAD detection)
- Rich event parsing — more complex than simple message-based formats
- Extended thinking content extraction (unique to Pi Agent)
- Model change tracking within sessions
- Scans for *_*.jsonl pattern in sessions directory

Follow migration pattern from FAD task 5.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- PiAgentConnector compiles in FAD
- All event types parsed correctly
- Extended thinking content preserved

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.14","title":"FAD: Move claude_code.rs connector to franken_agent_detection","description":"WHAT: Move src/connectors/claude_code.rs (1465 lines) to FAD.

CLAUDE CODE CONNECTOR: Parses Claude Code session files from ~/.claude/projects/. This is the PRIMARY connector (most sessions come from Claude Code). Handles .jsonl, .json, and .claude file formats. Extracts workspace, session_id, git_branch metadata. Computes timestamp bounds for incremental indexing.

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 1-4

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
- This is the highest-traffic connector — most users have the most Claude Code sessions
- Performance matters: scanning large numbers of JSONL files must be efficient
- The session directory structure is: ~/.claude/projects/<project-hash>/sessions/<session-id>/
- File format detection: .jsonl (primary), .json (older format), .claude (metadata)
- Metadata extraction includes git_branch, workspace path, session_id
- Incremental scan uses timestamp bounds to skip already-indexed sessions
- The connector_to_franken_slug mapping ('claude_code' -> 'claude') lives in this connector's detect() — after migration, this mapping is unnecessary since FAD owns both detection and scanning

PRIORITY: P1 because this is the most important connector by volume

Follow migration pattern from FAD task 5.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- ClaudeCodeConnector compiles in FAD
- All file formats (.jsonl, .json, .claude) parsed correctly
- Metadata extraction (workspace, session_id, git_branch) preserved
- Incremental scanning performance preserved
- cass imports from FAD, original removed","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:02:34.125245106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:40:51.444784165Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude_code","connector","fad","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:02:34.125245106Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:35.034872676Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:35.152910239Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:13:50.433536213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:35.264988837Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:35.380452062Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-6ecg5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:40:51.444654462Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

CLAUDE CODE CONNECTOR: Parses Claude Code session files from ~/.claude/projects/. This is the PRIMARY connector (most sessions come from Claude Code). Handles .jsonl, .json, and .claude file formats. Extracts workspace, session_id, git_branch metadata. Computes timestamp bounds for incremental indexing.

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 1-4

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.15","title":"FAD: Move gemini.rs connector to franken_agent_detection","description":"WHAT: Move src/connectors/gemini.rs (1594 lines) to FAD.

GEMINI CONNECTOR: Parses Gemini CLI session data from ~/.gemini/tmp/. Reads content.json files with a specific conversation structure. Handles metadata extraction and conversation reconstruction from Gemini's JSON format.

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 1-4

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
- content.json format is specific to Gemini CLI
- Conversation reconstruction from multiple JSON objects may be complex
- Metadata extraction specific to Gemini's response format
- Timestamp handling may differ from JSONL-based connectors

Follow migration pattern from FAD task 5.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- GeminiConnector compiles in FAD
- content.json parsing produces identical output

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.16","title":"FAD: Move chatgpt.rs connector to franken_agent_detection","description":"WHAT: Move src/connectors/chatgpt.rs (1748 lines) to FAD. This is the MOST DEPENDENCY-HEAVY connector due to encryption.

CHATGPT CONNECTOR: Parses ChatGPT desktop app conversations. Three format versions:
- v1: Unencrypted JSON (legacy)
- v2: AES-256-GCM encrypted with PBKDF2 key derivation
- v3: Updated encryption scheme

KEY COMPLEXITY: Encryption/decryption requires:
- aes-gcm crate for AES-256-GCM
- pbkdf2 for key derivation (600,000 iterations)
- macOS keychain access for encryption key extraction (security CLI)
- CHATGPT_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var fallback
- Ring or similar for cryptographic primitives

LOCATION: ~/Library/Application Support/com.openai.chat/ (macOS)

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 1-4, and specifically FAD task 3 (the 'chatgpt' feature flag with crypto deps)

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
- This connector is macOS-heavy (Application Support path, keychain)
- Crypto deps are heavy — MUST be behind 'chatgpt' feature flag
- Key extraction from macOS keychain uses subprocess (security find-generic-password)
- The encryption scheme must be preserved EXACTLY — any change breaks decryption
- v1/v2/v3 format detection is order-sensitive
- This is a good test of the feature gating system — users on Linux who don't use ChatGPT desktop should not pull in crypto deps

Follow migration pattern from FAD task 5, with extra attention to feature gating.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- ChatGPTConnector compiles in FAD behind 'chatgpt' feature
- All three format versions (v1, v2, v3) parse correctly
- Encryption/decryption produces identical results
- Keychain access works on macOS
- Env var fallback works on all platforms
- NOT compiled when 'chatgpt' feature is disabled
- cass imports from FAD, original removed","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:03:08.901969436Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:40:52.120501041Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chatgpt","connector","crypto","fad","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:03:08.901969436Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:35.952238682Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:36.065611943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:36.179618080Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:36.291842171Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-6ecg5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:40:52.120363814Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.17","title":"FAD: Move codex.rs connector to franken_agent_detection","description":"WHAT: Move src/connectors/codex.rs (1961 lines) to FAD.

CODEX CONNECTOR: Parses Codex (OpenAI) session files from ~/.codex/sessions/. Handles Rollout JSONL format (rollout-*.jsonl). UNIQUE FEATURE: Extracts token_count events and attaches token usage metadata to assistant turns.

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 1-4

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
- Rollout JSONL format is specific to Codex — events have types, some are token_count
- Token usage extraction and attachment to message turns is complex parsing logic
- Session file pattern: rollout-*.jsonl
- May have complex event interleaving logic
- CODEX_HOME env var support (already in FAD detection)

Follow migration pattern from FAD task 5.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- CodexConnector compiles in FAD
- Rollout event parsing produces identical output
- Token usage metadata correctly attached to assistant turns

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.22","title":"FAD: Clean up cass src/connectors/ — remove migrated files","description":"WHAT: Final cleanup of cass src/connectors/ after all FAD migrations are complete. Remove emptied files, update mod.rs to thin re-exports.

FILES TO REMOVE (after migration):
- src/connectors/vibe.rs
- src/connectors/clawdbot.rs
- src/connectors/aider.rs
- src/connectors/openclaw.rs
- src/connectors/copilot.rs
- src/connectors/factory.rs
- src/connectors/cline.rs
- src/connectors/amp.rs
- src/connectors/pi_agent.rs
- src/connectors/claude_code.rs
- src/connectors/gemini.rs
- src/connectors/chatgpt.rs
- src/connectors/codex.rs
- src/connectors/opencode.rs
- src/connectors/cursor.rs

FILE TO SLIM DOWN:
- src/connectors/mod.rs: Remove PathTrie, WorkspaceCache, helper functions, type definitions. Reduce to re-exports from FAD.

5. cargo check + clippy + test
6. Verify binary size reduction

IMPORTANT: Per AGENTS.md RULE 1, file deletion requires express permission. This task description serves as the specification — the implementing agent should confirm the deletion plan before executing.

- 15 connector files removed from cass

- All imports updated

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.23","title":"FAD: Move connector test fixtures from cass tests/fixtures/ to FAD","description":"WHAT: Move test fixture files from cass tests/fixtures/ that are used by connector tests to FAD.

WHY: When connectors move to FAD, their unit tests should move too. Tests need fixture data (sample JSONL files, sample databases, etc.) to run.

CURRENT LOCATION: cass tests/fixtures/ — contains sample session files in various formats for each agent type.

TARGET LOCATION: FAD tests/fixtures/ (mirror structure)

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 5-19 (connectors migrated — tests should move with connectors or after)

STEPS:
1. Identify which fixture files are used by connector-specific tests
2. Copy fixture files to FAD tests/fixtures/
3. Update connector test imports to reference new fixture paths
4. Also move/create integration tests in FAD tests/ for connector parsing
5. Keep cass integration tests that test the INDEXING of connector output (cass-specific)
6. Remove connector-specific fixtures from cass if no longer referenced

CONSIDERATIONS:
- Some fixtures may be used by both connector tests AND integration tests in cass
- Don't remove fixtures from cass prematurely — cass integration tests may still need them
- SQLite fixture databases (for Cursor, OpenCode) may need special handling
- Encrypted ChatGPT fixtures need the test encryption key

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All connector unit tests pass in FAD with their fixtures
- cass integration tests still have access to needed fixtures
- No test regression in either repo","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:04.444945066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:47.928484030Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fad","migration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.23","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:04.444945066Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.23","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:47.928352414Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.24","title":"FAD: Final verification — full test suite, feature gate audit, binary size","description":"WHAT: Comprehensive verification that the FAD migration is complete and correct.

DEPENDS ON: FAD tasks 22 (cleanup) AND 23 (fixtures)

VERIFICATION STEPS:

1. FAD FEATURE GATE AUDIT:
   - cargo check (default features = detection only) — should NOT pull in rusqlite, aes-gcm, etc.
   - cargo check --features connectors — basic connector support
   - cargo check --features all-connectors — everything
   - cargo check --features chatgpt — crypto deps only
   - cargo check --features cursor — rusqlite only
   - Verify each feature combination compiles correctly

2. FAD TEST SUITE:
   - cargo test --features all-connectors
   - cargo test --features detection (detection-only tests)
   - Verify test coverage for each connector

3. CASS INTEGRATION:
   - cargo test --all-features in cass
   - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings in both repos
   - cargo fmt --check in both repos

4. INDEXING PIPELINE VALIDATION:
   - Run cass index --full on a test dataset
   - Verify all connectors discover and parse sessions correctly
   - Compare output with pre-migration baseline (same conversations indexed)

5. BINARY SIZE AUDIT:
   - Build cass release binary
   - Compare with pre-migration binary size
   - Expected: significant reduction since connector code moved to FAD (shared library)

6. DEPENDENCY AUDIT:
   - FAD with default features: should have < 10 direct deps
   - FAD with all-connectors: should have ~15-20 direct deps
   - cass: should have fewer direct deps (connectors deps now transitive via FAD)

7. DOCUMENTATION:
   - Update FAD README with connector usage examples
   - Update FAD Cargo.toml with feature documentation
   - Update cass AGENTS.md project structure section
   - Update cass README connector table if needed

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All feature combinations compile
- All tests pass in both repos
- No clippy warnings
- Indexing pipeline produces identical results
- Binary size within 10% of pre-migration (should be smaller)
- Documentation updated","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:22.609223845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:14:10.236250127Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fad","migration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.24","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:22.609223845Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.24","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:48.043034666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.24","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:05:48.153096489Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.24","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:14:10.234084724Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.25","title":"FAD: Move token extraction utilities (normalize_model, extract tokens) to FAD","description":"WHAT: Move ~200 lines of token extraction logic from cass src/connectors/mod.rs to FAD. This code is TIGHTLY COUPLED to connectors and must move with them.

CODE TO MOVE:
1. normalize_model(raw: &str) -> ModelInfo — parses model strings into (family, tier, provider). Handles claude-3-opus, gpt-4-turbo, gemini-pro, etc.
2. extract_claude_code_tokens() — extracts token usage from Claude Code session metadata
3. extract_codex_tokens() — extracts token usage from Codex rollout events
4. estimate_tokens_from_content() — heuristic token count estimation (~4 chars/token)
5. TokenDataSource enum (Api vs Estimated)
6. ExtractedTokenUsage struct — input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, model, source
7. ModelInfo struct — family, tier, provider fields

WHY: These functions are connector-specific (they parse agent-specific token metadata). They're referenced by claude_code.rs and codex.rs connectors. Moving connectors without this code would break compilation.

DEPENDS ON: FAD task 1 (types — ExtractedTokenUsage may reference NormalizedMessage)

STEPS:
1. Read the token extraction section of mod.rs
2. Move to FAD src/connectors/token_extraction.rs
3. Ensure model normalization handles all 15 agent model formats
4. Move unit tests (if any)
5. Update connector imports

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Token extraction functions available from FAD
- Model normalization produces identical output for all known model strings
- extract_claude_code_tokens and extract_codex_tokens work identically
- Estimation heuristic unchanged","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:12:04.550855764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:13:46.107291772Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fad","migration","tokens"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.25","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:12:04.550855764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.25","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:13:46.105473389Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.26","title":"FAD: Create comprehensive connector integration test suite in FAD","description":"WHAT: Create a comprehensive integration test suite in FAD that validates every connector produces correct output. This runs in FAD (not cass) and proves connectors work standalone.

WHY: Currently 8 of 15 connectors have integration tests in cass tests/connector_*.rs. Two connectors (vibe, clawdbot) have NO integration tests at all. After migration, FAD must have its own test suite — we cannot rely on cass tests for FAD correctness.

EXISTING CASS TESTS TO PORT (8 files):
- tests/connector_aider.rs
- tests/connector_amp.rs
- tests/connector_claude.rs
- tests/connector_cline.rs
- tests/connector_codex.rs
- tests/connector_gemini.rs
- tests/connector_opencode.rs
- tests/connector_pi_agent.rs

TESTS TO CREATE FROM SCRATCH (7 connectors):
- vibe — parse messages.jsonl fixture
- clawdbot — parse session JSONL fixture
- copilot — parse VS Code extension storage fixture
- factory — parse session directory fixture
- chatgpt — test v1 unencrypted + v2/v3 encrypted fixtures
- cursor — test SQLite parsing with mock state.vscdb
- openclaw — parse agent directory fixture

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
1. Each connector test must validate:
   a. detect() returns correct result for mocked filesystem
   b. scan() parses fixture data into correct NormalizedConversation structure
   c. Message count matches expected
   d. Role assignment is correct (User/Agent/Tool/System)
   e. Timestamps are parsed correctly
   f. Workspace paths are extracted correctly
   g. Snippets (if any) have correct file_path and language

2. FIXTURE REQUIREMENTS:
   - Minimal but representative fixture for each connector
   - At least 3 messages (user + assistant + tool) per fixture
   - Cover edge cases: empty content, multi-byte unicode, very long messages
   - For SQLite connectors: include a test .db file
   - For ChatGPT: include test encrypted fixture with known test key

3. LOGGING:
   - Use tracing with test subscriber for detailed output
   - Log: connector name, fixture path, parse time, message count
   - On failure: log full expected vs actual diff for NormalizedConversation

4. E2E ROUND-TRIP TEST:
   - For each connector: fixture -> scan() -> serialize to JSON -> deserialize -> compare
   - Proves serialization compatibility

DEPENDS ON: FAD task 20 (all connectors migrated — tests need all connectors available)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- 15 connector test files in FAD tests/
- All 15 pass with 'cargo test --features all-connectors'
- Coverage for detect() AND scan() for every connector
- Fixture data included in FAD tests/fixtures/
- Round-trip serialization test passes for all connectors
- Test output includes timing and message count logging","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:12:25.227575208Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T19:14:05.738677870Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fad","migration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.26","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:12:25.227575208Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.26","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:14:01.903299776Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.26","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:14:05.736897387Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.27","title":"FAD: Add serialization compatibility tests for NormalizedConversation types","description":"WHAT: Before moving NormalizedConversation types to FAD, create golden-file serialization tests that PROVE the JSON shape is preserved exactly after the move.

WHY: NormalizedConversation, NormalizedMessage, and NormalizedSnippet are serialized to:
- SQLite storage (via serde_json::to_string and rmp_serde for MessagePack)
- Robot mode JSON output (via serde_json)
- Daemon protocol messages
- HTML export data
If the serde attributes or field order change during migration, existing data becomes unreadable. This is a SILENT DATA CORRUPTION risk.

APPROACH:
1. BEFORE moving types: Create golden JSON files for each type with known test data
2. AFTER moving types: Deserialize golden files with the new FAD types and verify field-by-field

GOLDEN FILES TO CREATE (in tests/fixtures/golden/):
1. normalized_conversation.json — Full conversation with all fields populated
2. normalized_message.json — Message with role, content, timestamps, extras, snippets
3. normalized_snippet.json — Snippet with file_path, line numbers, language, text
4. message_role_variants.json — All MessageRole variants (User, Agent, Tool, System, Other)
5. scan_context.json — ScanContext with all options set

TESTS TO WRITE:
1. serialize_golden_conversation() — Serialize known data, compare byte-for-byte with golden file
2. deserialize_golden_conversation() — Deserialize golden file, verify all fields
3. roundtrip_conversation() — Serialize -> Deserialize -> compare
4. messagepack_roundtrip() — Same but with rmp_serde (for SQLite binary format)
5. backwards_compat() — Old JSON (from cass) deserializes with new FAD types

DEPENDS ON: Must be done BEFORE FAD task 1 (creating golden files requires the OLD types still in cass). Then run AGAIN after FAD task 1 to verify.

STEPS:
1. Write golden file generation script using current cass types
2. Create golden JSON files with representative data
3. Write deserialization tests that validate against golden files
4. Run tests BEFORE migration (must pass — establishes baseline)
5. Run tests AFTER FAD task 1 migration (must still pass — proves compatibility)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Golden files created for all 5 types
- Serialization tests pass before AND after migration
- Both JSON and MessagePack formats tested
- Field order in JSON is preserved (use #[serde(rename)] if needed)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:13:00.410287041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T20:13:50.395942473Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T20:13:50.395862473Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fad","migration","serialization","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.27","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:13:00.410287041Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.27","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3r4jg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:13:12.895935204Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.3","title":"FAD: Add feature-gated heavy dependencies for connector support","description":"WHAT: Add all dependencies needed by the 15 connectors to FAD Cargo.toml, behind feature gates so detection-only users stay lightweight.

CURRENT FAD DEPS: serde, serde_json, chrono, dirs (minimal)

NEW DEPS NEEDED:
- rusqlite (bundled) — Cursor and OpenCode parse SQLite databases
- aes-gcm + pbkdf2 + argon2 — ChatGPT v2/v3 encrypted conversations
- walkdir — filesystem traversal for session discovery
- glob — pattern matching for session file discovery
- anyhow — error handling in scan() methods
- tracing — structured logging throughout connectors
- regex — pattern matching in various parsers
- blake3 — content hashing for deduplication
- unicode-normalization — NFC text canonicalization
- thiserror — typed error variants

FEATURE GATE DESIGN:
[features]
default = ['detection']  # Keep current behavior as default
detection = []  # Filesystem probing only (current FAD)
connectors = ['dep:rusqlite', 'dep:walkdir', 'dep:glob', 'dep:anyhow', 'dep:tracing', 'dep:regex', 'dep:blake3', 'dep:unicode-normalization', 'dep:thiserror']
chatgpt = ['connectors', 'dep:aes-gcm', 'dep:pbkdf2', 'dep:argon2']  # Heavy crypto only for ChatGPT
cursor = ['connectors', 'dep:rusqlite']  # SQLite for Cursor
opencode = ['connectors', 'dep:rusqlite']  # SQLite for OpenCode
all-connectors = ['connectors', 'chatgpt', 'cursor', 'opencode']

WHY FEATURE GATES: Without feature gates, anyone who just needs detection (does this agent exist?) would pull in rusqlite, ONNX crypto, etc. That violates the principle of minimal dependencies.

STEPS:
1. Audit all 15 connectors to catalog every external crate they use
2. Design feature gate hierarchy (which deps serve which connectors)
3. Add deps to FAD Cargo.toml with optional = true
4. Create feature definitions
5. Add cfg attributes to connector modules
6. Verify: 'cargo check' with default features = detection only
7. Verify: 'cargo check --features all-connectors' = everything

CONSIDERATIONS:
- rusqlite with 'bundled' feature compiles SQLite from C source — this adds build time. Consider making the bundled feature optional.
- aes-gcm + pbkdf2 are security-critical — pin to exact versions, not wildcard
- Some deps may already be transitive (e.g., serde_json through serde)
- Test that 'default' feature (detection only) compiles without any connector deps
- The 'connectors' feature should be the common base, with per-connector features adding heavy deps

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- 'cargo check' with default features succeeds (detection only)
- 'cargo check --features all-connectors' succeeds
- No unnecessary deps pulled in for detection-only use
- Feature documentation in Cargo.toml comments","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:00:04.791375688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T20:10:23.436237646Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T20:10:23.436212949Z","close_reason":"Feature-gated dependencies added to FAD Cargo.toml. Features: default (detection only, 4 deps), connectors (7 optional deps for all 15 connectors), chatgpt (aes-gcm + base64), cursor (rusqlite bundled), all-connectors (everything). Verified: cargo check (default) clean, cargo check --features all-connectors clean, clippy clean both configs, cass still compiles.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deps","fad","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:00:04.791375688Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u2f7.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3r4jg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:13:12.548149509Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
